text:
AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-02

今日结论：
AI Agent frameworks remain the dominant category, with Hermes WebUI gaining 1,940 stars in 24 hours and Supermemory adding 1,020, both showing sustained hot trends. The category's average daily delta of 2,407 stars far exceeds others, indicating strong developer interest in building agentic systems. Meanwhile, Anthropic's confidential S-1 submission to the SEC, which hit #1 on Hacker News with 469 votes, suggests a major funding event that could reshape the competitive dynamics of the AI industry. This cross-source signal (HN + GitHub) reinforces the narrative that agent frameworks are the current focus, but the IPO news may shift attention toward infrastructure and enterprise AI in the coming days.

今日精选项目
1. nesquena/hermes-webui
   GitHub: https://github.com/nesquena/hermes-webui
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 11,581 (24h +1940, 7d 暂无基线)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1x (low, 前 3 日均值 n/a)
   Score: 73 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A lightweight, dark-themed web UI for Hermes Agent that mirrors the CLI experience with persistent memory, scheduling, and multi-platform access.
   Why worth watching: The repo gained 1,940 stars in the last 24 hours, reaching 11,581 total stars with 1,471 forks. The acceleration is low (1.0), but the sustained_hot trend and recent release within 30 days indicate strong ongoing interest. The project is actively maintained with commits within 7 days.
   Developer takeaway: Signals growing demand for self-hosted, persistent AI agents that don't reset context between sessions. The Python ecosystem and provider-agnostic design lower adoption barriers for developers building agentic workflows.
   Problem solved: Provides a browser-based interface for Hermes Agent, eliminating the need for terminal-only interaction while retaining full CLI parity, enabling mobile and remote access via SSH tunnel.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and active commits suggest continuous improvement, but no specific external event (e.g., HN front page, major announcement) is evident from the metadata.
   Target users: Developers and power users who want a self-hosted AI assistant with memory, scheduling, and multi-platform access, accessible from a browser or phone.
   Risk notes: Project is tied to Hermes Agent; if Hermes loses momentum, the UI's value diminishes. Native Windows support is limited (WSL2 recommended). 224 open issues suggest active development but also potential bugs.
   LLM confidence: high

   Problem solved: It provides a low-code agent builder, workflow management, and pre-built agents to automate tasks like content generation and social media posting, reducing manual effort.

3. D4Vinci/Scrapling

   Category: MCP / Tool Calling
   Stars: 58,300 (24h +2125, 7d 暂无基线)

   Score: 60 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An adaptive web scraping framework that auto-relocates elements when sites change, bypasses anti-bot systems, and scales from single requests to full crawls.
   Why worth watching: With 57,984 stars and a 24-hour delta of 1,809, the project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is low (1), indicating steady organic interest rather than a sudden spike. This consistent attention suggests the project has become a go-to tool in the web scraping community.
   Developer takeaway: For AI developers, Scrapling's MCP server and agent skill directory signal a shift toward making web scraping a first-class capability for AI agents, enabling autonomous data collection without constant manual selector updates.
   Problem solved: Web scrapers break when websites update their HTML structure, and anti-bot measures like Cloudflare Turnstile block traditional tools. Scrapling's parser learns from changes and automatically finds elements again, while its fetchers handle anti-bot bypass out of the box.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and a release within 30 days show active maintenance, but no external buzz or specific event is identifiable.
   Target users: Python developers building web scrapers, data extraction pipelines, or AI agents that need to collect data from modern websites with anti-bot protections.
   Risk notes: The project is relatively new (first release likely in 2025) and may have undiscovered edge cases. Reliance on anti-bot bypass techniques could be fragile if target sites update their defenses. The README includes sponsored proxy services, which may introduce bias in recommendations.
   LLM confidence: high

4. supermemoryai/supermemory
   GitHub: https://github.com/supermemoryai/supermemory
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 24,113 (24h +1020, 7d 暂无基线)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1x (low, 前 3 日均值 n/a)
   Score: 58 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Supermemory is a state-of-the-art memory and context engine for AI, providing persistent memory, user profiles, hybrid search, and connectors via a single API.
   Why worth watching: With 23,845 stars, a 24-hour star delta of 752, and a sustained hot trend, Supermemory is experiencing strong, consistent growth. The project has recent commits and a release within 30 days, indicating active development. Its #1 ranking on three major AI memory benchmarks (LongMemEval, LoCoMo, ConvoMem) and the release of plugins for Claude Code, OpenCode, and others are driving attention.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward commoditized memory infrastructure for AI. Developers no longer need to build custom memory solutions; they can rely on a specialized, benchmark-optimized service. The rise of MCP and plugin integrations suggests memory will become a standard layer in AI toolchains.
   Problem solved: AI assistants forget everything between conversations. Supermemory automatically extracts facts, builds user profiles, handles knowledge updates and contradictions, forgets expired information, and delivers the right context at the right time.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent activity includes new plugins for AI coding tools and continued benchmark leadership.
   Target users: AI developers building agents or apps that need persistent user memory, context, and RAG; also end-users who want their AI assistants to remember preferences and past conversations.
   Risk notes: As a hosted service, there are potential concerns around data privacy, vendor lock-in, and latency. The project is open-source but the core memory engine may be proprietary. Competition from other memory solutions (Mem0, Zep) could fragment the market.
   LLM confidence: high

   Why worth watching: The repo shows a sudden breakout with a 5.8x acceleration ratio and a 24-hour star delta of 27, indicating a spike in attention. With 42,438 stars and 4,849 forks, it's already a major project, but the recent acceleration suggests new interest, likely from recent releases like Muon Optimizer support or SDMA for AMD GPUs.
   Developer takeaway: This signals that the community is increasingly focused on making large-scale training accessible on modest hardware, and DeepSpeed is leading that shift.
   Problem solved: DeepSpeed addresses the challenges of training and deploying large-scale deep learning models by providing memory and speed optimizations (e.g., ZeRO, 3D parallelism) that enable billion-parameter models on limited hardware.
   What changed: Recent commits and a release within 30 days, plus news about Muon Optimizer and SDMA support, likely drove the spike. The project also has active community engagement with office hours and multiple integrations.
   Target users: Deep learning engineers, researchers, and organizations training or fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) or other large neural networks.
   Risk notes: DeepSpeed has a steep learning curve and may require custom CUDA extensions. It also has many open issues (1,292), indicating potential bugs or feature requests. Compatibility with non-NVIDIA hardware (AMD, Intel) is improving but may still have rough edges.

It ranks top 2 in Agents, top 3 in LLMs, and top 3 in CLI by velocity on oosmetrics.

   Also on: GitHub

   Target users: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding agent; teams looking for a cost-effective, configurable alternative to GitHub Copilot or Cursor; Go enthusiasts and CLI tool users.
   Risk notes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-caching behavior; the project is still in early stages (1.0 rewrite, not yet on npm). 466 open issues may indicate growing pains or active feature requests. Competition from established agents (e.g., Claude Code, Copilot) could limit adoption.

   Target users: Developers and designers who want a local-first, open-source design tool that integrates with their existing coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) and supports BYOK for models. Also suitable for teams needing a self-hostable alternative to Claude Design or Figma.

Model & Demo Signals
1. Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
   Source: arxiv | Type: paper
   URL: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.02578v1
   Signal captured
   Category: cs.CV
   Summary: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response text instead of their own visual perception, leading to inconsistent and non-verifiable evaluations. To address this issue, we introduce the Perceptually Perturbed Judgment Dataset, which constructs minimally edited counterfactual responses that isolate perceptual errors and enable verifiable supervision. Building on this dataset, we develop a unified training framework that combines a structured GRPO-based reward with a batch-ranking objective, achieving coherent global ordering without explicit pairwise labels. Experiments across diverse MLLM-as-a-Judge benchmarks show that our approach substantially improves perceptual fidelity, ranking coherence, and alignment with human evaluation. Our results establish a scalable and generalizable pathway for training multimodal judges that are perceptually grounded, interpretable, and robust to visual-reasoning conflicts.
   Why it matters: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, alignment, benchmark, evaluation

2. ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
   Source: arxiv | Type: paper
   URL: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.02576v1
   Signal captured
   Category: cs.CV
   Summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential. To reduce inter-task interference and promote collaboration, recent methods often employ sparse architectures like Mixture of LoRA Experts with image-text similarity routing. However, tasks with distinct response structures could share highly similar visual-linguistic semantics and thus be wrongly routed to the same expert; image-text similarity alone is insufficient for reliable task assignment. For example, an expert in a grounding task requiring coordinate prediction may be biased toward producing short textual answers after learning semantically similar VQA tasks. This format-blind task assignment integrates heterogeneous response types into shared parameters, inducing gradient interference and ineffective expert collaboration. To address this problem, we propose ProtoAda, a prototype-guided adaptive tuning framework. ProtoAda introduces format-aware task prototypes to align task assignment and routing with both task semantics and output structure, and further consolidates format-compatible updates in a geometry-aware manner to effectively reuse and progressively refine existing parameters. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that ProtoAda achieves superior performance, especially on tasks whose answer structures are easily corrupted by sequential tuning.
   Why it matters: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, benchmark

3. AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
   Source: arxiv | Type: paper
   URL: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.02569v1
   Signal captured
   Category: cs.CV
   Summary: Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame changes. We call this interface a \emph{predictive visual code}, and instantiate it for video MLLMs as \textbf{AdaCodec}. AdaCodec spends full visual tokens on a reference frame only when its conditional predictive cost is high; otherwise, it encodes inter-frame changes, including motion and prediction residuals, as compact P-tokens. Across all eleven benchmarks, AdaCodec improves over the Qwen3-VL-8B per-frame RGB baseline at a matched visual-token budget. Even at $1/7$ the budget, AdaCodec with 32k tokens surpasses the 224k baseline on all long-video benchmarks; on five general-video benchmarks, it raises the average score while substantially cutting time-to-first-token from 9.26s to 1.62s.
   Why it matters: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag, benchmark

4. ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
   Source: arxiv | Type: paper
   URL: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.02568v1
   Signal captured
   Category: cs.AI
   Summary: Clinical practice is not the selection of an answer from enumerated options: a physician gathers heterogeneous information incrementally and commits to sequential, irreversible decisions under uncertainty. Static benchmarks cannot probe and existing interactive medical benchmarks each compromise on at least one of them. We present ClinEnv, an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions under a paradigm we term Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation. Each case is automatically constructed into an ordered sequence of decision stages; at every stage the model must actively query four specialized agents before committing to medications, procedures, and diagnoses. ClinEnv scores both what the model decides, through deterministic ontology-grounded matching, and how it gathers information. Across seven models, the strongest reaches only 0.31 decision F1, and outcome quality is sharply decoupled from process quality. Difficulty concentrates in management decisions and later stages, where models recover discharge diagnoses far more reliably than management actions (0.51 vs. 0.17 F1) and continue to issue redundant queries as cases progress. ClinEnv makes this information-acquisition gap, invisible to outcome-only evaluation, directly measurable.
   Why it matters: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, benchmark, evaluation

5. Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
   Source: arxiv | Type: paper
   URL: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.02562v1
   Signal captured
   Category: cs.RO
   Summary: Autonomous robots that interact with people must make safe and efficient decisions under human-induced uncertainty, such as their preferences, goals, competency, and willingness to cooperate. Safety filters are a popular approach for ensuring safety in interactive robotics, since their modular design separates safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely around people with minimal impact on task efficiency. While traditional safety filters typically operate only in the physical space, neglecting the robot's ability to learn and adapt online, the recently proposed belief-space safety filter (BeliefSF) reasons about robot safety in closed-loop with runtime inference that actively reduces the robot's uncertainty online, thereby reducing conservativeness in filtering. However, providing formal safety guarantees for robots deploying BeliefSF remains a significant challenge due to errors in runtime inference and neural approximation of safety filters required to handle the high dimensionality of belief spaces. In this paper, we propose an algorithmic approach to certify high-probability safety of BeliefSF using conformal prediction, while explicitly accounting for the reliability of the robot's runtime inference module. Our method leverages the structure of belief-space safety filtering by focusing verification on a region where inference is expected to be reliable. It preserves the simplicity and sample complexity of standard conformal prediction, yet can certify a substantially less conservative safety filter. Through a simulated human-vehicle interaction benchmark, we show that our approach verifies a significantly more permissive belief-space safety filter than a standard conformal prediction baseline.
   Why it matters: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, safety, benchmark

1. 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/Khazix0918/status/2061669881725309048
   Signal captured
   Category: tools
   Summary: 作者受一条推文启发，使用Codex对自己的MacBook进行了只读存储分析，发现了B站缓存视频等大量可清理空间（激进方案超140G）。为替代收费软件CleanMyMac，作者制作并开源了一个跨平台（支持Mac/Windows）的AI清理skill。该skill会扫描文件并生成可交互的HTML报告，通过三色分级（绿灯可放心清理、黄灯需人工判断、红灯禁止动）直观展示，并提供安全执行按钮。实测清理后释放了近120G空间，相比CleanMyMac仅扫描出的15.8G，其信息更透明、建议更详细。

2. 阶跃星辰Step 3.7 Flash发布，专为高效推理设计
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend

4. Anthropic 开发者分享 Claude Code「理解验证」工作流
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/shao__meng/status/2061612512580346245
   Signal captured
   Category: tools
   Summary: Anthropic 核心开发者分享了一套用于 Claude Code 的「理解验证」工作流。该工作流将 AI 定位为"高效且睿智的教师"，成功标准不仅是完成任务，更是确保人类对问题、方案及影响有可复述、可辩护的掌握。它通过增量教学、用户复述、清单+测验等方式，围绕问题域、方案域和语境域三条轴线展开，具体包含8个可执行步骤，强调在进入下一阶段前需确认用户已真正理解。此工作流旨在对抗长会话中人类易沦为"审批按钮"的"智能体黑箱"问题，强制沉淀决策上下文，实现可审计的深度理解。

6. Sam Altman强调AI发展应以人为本
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/rohanpaul_ai/status/2061586179292831774
   Signal captured
   Category: tools
   Summary: Sam Altman在采访中表示，AI不应被设计为追求脱离人类需求的目标，人类必须始终处于AI发展的中心。他批判了行业内"AI将摧毁大量工作"等言论，认为人们担忧的并非AI带来的好处，而是自身在未来的角色、经济前景与自主权。他指出，AI行业的失败在于未能清晰解释人类如何在每一步保持对未来的控制权，以及如何在AI时代继续拥有充实、有意义的生活。
